locked_facts.energy_formula: W = P * t = V * I * t
locked_facts.joule_heating: heat_produced_H = I^2 * R * t (Joule's law of heating)

ncert_truth_statements: Electric power is the rate at which electrical energy is consumed or delivered: P = VI = I²R = V²/R
The electrical energy consumed by a device in time t is W = Pt = VIt, measured in joules in the SI system
The commercial unit of electrical energy is the kilowatt-hour (kWh); 1 kWh = 3.6 × 10⁶ J
Joule's law of heating states that the heat produced in a conductor is H = I²Rt, proportional to the square of the current, the resistance, and the time
In a purely resistive circuit, all electrical energy supplied by the source is converted into heat energy in the resistors
